what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon named collapse, drawn as four L-shaped corner marks using the current text color.
observations: Uses an inline SVG with viewBox "0 0 24 24".
Sets fill to "none" and stroke to "currentColor".
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Contains one path element with four disconnected L-shaped subpaths.
standouts: Single path encodes all four corner marks.
The icon inherits color from its rendering context.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions with a 24x24 viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke, and rounded stroke styling.
Keeps the asset self-contained with no external references or metadata.
notes: A minimal collapse-style outline SVG composed of four corner brackets.
questions: From this file alone, should the visual be interpreted as collapse, exit fullscreen, or a generic corner-control icon?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a collapse icon, and the drawing presents four inward-facing corner marks consistent with that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears intended to serve as a reusable UI icon for a collapse or reduce-view action while adapting to surrounding text color.
contribution: Contributes a compact, color-inheriting collapse icon asset with simple stroke-based geometry suitable for reuse in icon-led controls.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 SVG collapse icon made from four rounded, currentColor, L-shaped corner strokes in a single path.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for providing a reusable collapse-control icon within a design system asset set.
